title: Vaidyanath Jyotirlinga
tagline: Vaidyanath Jyotirlinga — spiritual town of Jharkhand

overview.short: Baidyanath Temple (IAST: Baidyãnath) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva.
overview.description: Baidyanath Temple (IAST: Baidyãnath) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva. It is located in Deoghar, in the Santhal Parganas division of the Indian state of Jharkhand. The temple complex comprises the central shrine of Baba Baidyanath along with 21 additional temples. It is significant to the Hindu sects of Shaivism as this temple is referred to as one of the twelve Jyotirlingas.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Shravani Mela
topPlaces.description: Shravani Mela is a month-long Hindu festival observed in the month of Shravan (July–August) at the Baba Baidyanath Dham temple in Deoghar, Jharkhand, India. This festival is one of the largest religious gatherings in India, attracting millions of devotees, primarily the Kanwariyas, who undertake a pilgrimage to offer holy water from the Ganges to Lord Shiva.

topPlaces.name: Baidyanath Dham (Nepal)
topPlaces.description: Baidyanath Dham, one of the four Shrines of Nepal, is a shrine of Lord Shiva located in Achham District in the confluence of the Budhi Ganga and Saraswati rivers in Achham district. The shrine has a school to teach Ved called Ved Bidyashram.

topPlaces.name: Deoghar (community development block)
topPlaces.description: Deoghar is a community development block that forms an administrative division in the Deoghar subdivision, of the Deoghar district, Jharkhand state, India.

topPlaces.name: Naulakha Temple, Deoghar
topPlaces.description: The Naulakha Temple is a Hindu temple located in Deoghar, Jharkhand, India. It is dedicated to the Hindu deities Radha and Krishna.

topPlaces.name: Deoghar
topPlaces.description: Deoghar (pronounced Devghar) is a city and a municipal corporation in Deoghar district in the Indian state of Jharkhand. It is also the administrative headquarters of Deoghar district.

topPlaces.name: Ramakrishna Mission Vidyapith, Deoghar
topPlaces.description: Ramakrishna Mission Vidyapith, Deoghar is a residential boys' senior secondary school in Deoghar, Jharkhand, India, established in 1922. It is the oldest institute of Ramakrishna Mission, and used to be visited by brother disciples of Swami Vivekananda.

topPlaces.name: Satsang Ashram
topPlaces.description: Satsang Ashram is the headquarters of the Satsang movement started by Thakur Anukulchandra in India and across the world. The Satsang Ashram has become an of attraction in Deoghar for all kinds of people in the society.

topPlaces.name: Satar, Deoghar
topPlaces.description: Satar (24.4865°N 86.6520°E / 24.4865; 86.6520) is a village in Deoghar district of Jharkhand state of India.

items.note: Shravani Mela is a month-long Hindu festival observed in the month of Shravan (July–August) at the Baba Baidyanath Dham temple in Deoghar, Jharkhand, India. This festival is one of the largest religious gatherings in India, attracting millions of devotees, primarily the Kanwariyas, who undertake a pilgrimage to offer holy water from the Ganges to Lord Shiva.

Baidyanath Dham, one of the four Shrines of Nepal, is a shrine of Lord Shiva located in Achham District in the confluence of the Budhi Ganga and Saraswati rivers in Achham district. The shrine has a school to teach Ved called Ved Bidyashram.
Deoghar is a community development block that forms an administrative division in the Deoghar subdivision, of the Deoghar district, Jharkhand state, India.
The Naulakha Temple is a Hindu temple located in Deoghar, Jharkhand, India. It is dedicated to the Hindu deities Radha and Krishna.
Deoghar (pronounced Devghar) is a city and a municipal corporation in Deoghar district in the Indian state of Jharkhand. It is also the administrative headquarters of Deoghar district.

Ramakrishna Mission Vidyapith, Deoghar is a residential boys' senior secondary school in Deoghar, Jharkhand, India, established in 1922. It is the oldest institute of Ramakrishna Mission, and used to be visited by brother disciples of Swami Vivekananda.

Satsang Ashram is the headquarters of the Satsang movement started by Thakur Anukulchandra in India and across the world. The Satsang Ashram has become an of attraction in Deoghar for all kinds of people in the society.
Satar (24.4865°N 86.6520°E / 24.4865; 86.6520) is a village in Deoghar district of Jharkhand state of India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vaidyanath Jyotirlinga?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vaidyanath Jyotirlinga is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vaidyanath Jyotirlinga?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Gaya International Airport (179 km) and the nearest railway station is Dhanbad Junction (82 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vaidyanath Jyotirlinga?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vaidyanath Jyotirlinga at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vaidyanath Jyotirlinga?
faq.a: Stays in Vaidyanath Jyotirlinga start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vaidyanath Jyotirlinga?
faq.a: The top attractions are Shravani Mela, Baidyanath Dham (Nepal), Deoghar (community development block), Naulakha Temple, Deoghar, Deoghar.
